resposta:
solution: ```python
class Solution:
    def originalDigits(self, s: str) -> str:
        from collections import Counter
        cnt = Counter(s)
        digit_counts = [0] * 10
        
        # 0: z appears only in zero
        digit_counts[0] = cnt['z']
        # 2: w appears only in two
        digit_counts[2] = cnt['w']
        # 4: u appears only in four
        digit_counts[4] = cnt['u']
        # 6: x appears only in six
        digit_counts[6] = cnt['x']
        # 8: g appears only in eight
        digit_counts[8] = cnt['g']
        
        # 3: h appears only in three and eight (eight already counted)
        digit_counts[3] = cnt['h'] - digit_counts[8]
        # 5: f appears only in four and five (four already counted)
        digit_counts[5] = cnt['f'] - digit_counts[4]
        # 7: s appears only in six and seven (six already counted)
        digit_counts[7] = cnt['s'] - digit_counts[6]
        
        # 1: o appears in zero, one, two, four (zero, two, four already counted)
        digit_counts[1] = cnt['o'] - digit_counts[0] - digit_counts[2] - digit_counts[4]
        # 9: i appears in five, six, eight, nine (five, six, eight already counted)
        digit_counts[9] = cnt['i'] - digit_counts[5] - digit_counts[6] - digit_counts[8]
        
        return ''.join(str(i) * digit_counts[i] for i in range(10))
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Count character frequencies once, then determine each digit count by unique letter subtraction. Runs in linear time with constant extra space.

class Solution:
    def originalDigits(self, s: str) -> str:
        cnt = Counter(s)
        digit_counts = [0] * 10
        
        # 0: z appears only in zero
        digit_counts[0] = cnt['z']
        # 2: w appears only in two
        digit_counts[2] = cnt['w']
        # 4: u appears only in four
        digit_counts[4] = cnt['u']
        # 6: x appears only in six
        digit_counts[6] = cnt['x']
        # 8: g appears only in eight
        digit_counts[8] = cnt['g']
        
        # 3: h appears only in three and eight (eight already counted)
        digit_counts[3] = cnt['h'] - digit_counts[8]
        # 5: f appears only in four and five (four already counted)
        digit_counts[5] = cnt['f'] - digit_counts[4]
        # 7: s appears only in six and seven (six already counted)
        digit_counts[7] = cnt['s'] - digit_counts[6]
        
        # 1: o appears in zero, one, two, four (zero, two, four already counted)
        digit_counts[1] = cnt['o'] - digit_counts[0] - digit_counts[2] - digit_counts[4]
        # 9: i appears in five, six, eight, nine (five, six, eight already counted)
        digit_counts[9] = cnt['i'] - digit_counts[5] - digit_counts[6] - digit_counts[8]
        
        return ''.join(str(i) * digit_counts[i] for i in range(10))

class Solution:
    def originalDigits(self, s: str) -> str:
        from collections import Counter
        cnt = Counter(s)
        digit_counts = [0] * 10
        
        # 0: z appears only in zero
        digit_counts[0] = cnt['z']
        # 2: w appears only in two
        digit_counts[2] = cnt['w']
        # 4: u appears only in four
        digit_counts[4] = cnt['u']
        # 6: x appears only in six
        digit_counts[6] = cnt['x']
        # 8: g appears only in eight
        digit_counts[8] = cnt['g']
        
        # 3: h appears only in three and eight (eight already counted)
        digit_counts[3] = cnt['h'] - digit_counts[8]
        # 5: f appears only in four and five (four already counted)
        digit_counts[5] = cnt['f'] - digit_counts[4]
        # 7: s appears only in six and seven (six already counted)
        digit_counts[7] = cnt['s'] - digit_counts[6]
        
        # 1: o appears in zero, one, two, four (zero, two, four already counted)
        digit_counts[1] = cnt['o'] - digit_counts[0] - digit_counts[2] - digit_counts[4]
        # 9: i appears in five, six, eight, nine (five, six, eight already counted)
        digit_counts[9] = cnt['i'] - digit_counts[5] - digit_counts[6] - digit_counts[8]
        
        return ''.join(str(i) * digit_counts[i] for i in range(10))
